meta.title: Help for Homebuyers Program Watchlist
meta.methodology: Consumer-facing watchlist of homebuyer and homeowner assistance programs. Dollar values are included only when visible on an official program page reviewed this session; otherwise benefit_amount remains null and the page renders VERIFY.

programs.name: CHFA Down Payment Assistance Grant
programs.plain_summary: CHFA borrowers may pair a CHFA first mortgage with grant help for down payment or closing costs. The grant does not have to be repaid, but borrowers must qualify through a CHFA participating lender.
programs.who_its_for: Homebuyers using an eligible CHFA first mortgage.
programs.what_its_worth: Up to the lesser of $25,000 or 3% of the first mortgage.
programs.how_to_start: Contact a CHFA participating lender and ask whether the grant option fits your loan.
programs.source_note: CHFA lists a grant up to the lesser of $25,000 or 3% of the first mortgage, with no repayment required.

programs.name: CHFA Second Mortgage Loan
programs.plain_summary: CHFA borrowers may use a second mortgage for down payment or closing costs. Repayment is deferred until events such as payoff, sale, refinance, or the home no longer being the borrower’s primary residence.
programs.who_its_for: Homebuyers using an eligible CHFA first mortgage who prefer deferred repayment assistance.
programs.what_its_worth: Up to the lesser of $25,000 or 4% of the first mortgage.
programs.how_to_start: Ask a CHFA participating lender to compare the grant and second-mortgage options.
programs.source_note: CHFA lists a second mortgage up to the lesser of $25,000 or 4% of the first mortgage, with repayment deferred until triggering events.

programs.name: CHFA FirstGeneration
programs.plain_summary: CHFA FirstGeneration helps eligible first-generation, first-time buyers pair a CHFA mortgage with down payment assistance. CHFA says the program is ongoing and starts through a participating lender.
programs.who_its_for: Eligible first-generation homebuyers using a CHFA first mortgage.
programs.what_its_worth: $25,000 down payment assistance with deferred repayment.
programs.how_to_start: Contact a CHFA participating lender and ask about CHFA FirstGeneration.
programs.source_note: CHFA describes $25,000 down payment assistance and deferred repayment for first-generation buyers who use a CHFA first mortgage.

programs.name: CHFA Home Purchase Loan Programs
programs.plain_summary: CHFA home purchase loans are offered through participating lenders. They can be paired with CHFA grants or second mortgages, and CHFA requires approved homebuyer education before closing.
programs.who_its_for: Colorado buyers who meet CHFA credit, income, lender, and education requirements.
programs.what_its_worth: VERIFY: loan terms vary by product and lender; this page does not quote a benefit amount.
programs.how_to_start: Read CHFA’s loan requirements, complete homebuyer education, then contact a CHFA participating lender.
programs.source_note: CHFA lists home purchase loan programs, grants, and second mortgage loans through participating lenders, with general requirements verified by the lender.

programs.name: CHFA Mortgage Credit Certificate for Current Holders
programs.plain_summary: CHFA says it has not issued new Mortgage Credit Certificates since 2018. Existing certificate holders may still be able to claim or reissue benefits if they meet CHFA and IRS rules.
programs.who_its_for: Current CHFA MCC holders, not new applicants.
programs.what_its_worth: VERIFY: current-holder tax benefits depend on the original certificate and federal tax situation.
programs.how_to_start: Current holders should review CHFA’s MCC holder page or contact CHFA before filing or refinancing.
programs.source_note: CHFA states it has not issued MCCs since 2018 and directs current holders to Form 8396 and CHFA reissuance materials.

programs.name: metroDPA
programs.plain_summary: metroDPA offers Front Range borrowers a fixed-rate mortgage option with down payment assistance. The assistance is a zero percent second mortgage, and buyers must meet credit, income, property, and education requirements.
programs.who_its_for: Eligible Colorado Front Range buyers using participating metroDPA lenders.
programs.what_its_worth: VERIFY: assistance is a percentage of the note amount, but this page does not quote a current percentage.
programs.how_to_start: Contact an approved metroDPA lender and ask for the current assistance options and income limits.
programs.source_note: metroDPA describes fixed-rate loans with optional down payment assistance as a zero percent second mortgage with no scheduled payments.

programs.name: CHAC Down Payment Assistance Loans
programs.plain_summary: CHAC provides education, counseling, and flexible loans for low- and moderate-income first-time buyers. The loans can help with down payment and closing costs throughout Colorado.
programs.who_its_for: Low- and moderate-income first-time homebuyers in Colorado.
programs.what_its_worth: VERIFY: CHAC points borrowers to loan descriptions and income limits rather than one universal amount.
programs.how_to_start: Review CHAC’s borrower materials or email CHAC’s new-loan team before applying.
programs.source_note: CHAC describes low-interest, flexible loans for down payment and closing cost assistance throughout Colorado.

programs.name: Colorado Senior and Disabled Veteran Homestead Exemption
programs.plain_summary: Colorado’s homestead exemption can reduce property taxes for qualifying seniors and disabled veterans. Eligibility and benefit details are administered through state and county property-tax channels.
programs.who_its_for: Eligible Colorado senior homeowners, disabled veterans, and qualifying survivors.
programs.what_its_worth: VERIFY: confirm the current exempt value and application rules with the Division of Property Taxation or county assessor.
programs.how_to_start: Start with the Colorado Division of Property Taxation page, then follow your county assessor’s application instructions.
programs.source_note: Official Colorado Division of Property Taxation page; benefit amount left null because this PR did not verify a current dollar value on a fetchable page.

programs.name: Federal 25C Energy Efficient Home Improvement Credit
programs.plain_summary: The federal 25C credit helped some owners pay for qualified energy improvements. Under Public Law 119-21, the credit is not allowed for property placed in service after December 31, 2025.
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners reviewing pre-2026 energy-improvement tax records.
programs.what_its_worth: Expired for post-2025 property placed in service; consult a tax professional for prior-year claims.
programs.how_to_start: Use IRS guidance for prior-year questions; do not assume this credit applies to new 2026 work.
programs.source_note: IRS FAQ states section 25C is not allowed for property placed in service after December 31, 2025.

programs.name: Federal 25D Residential Clean Energy Credit
programs.plain_summary: The federal 25D credit helped some owners pay for residential clean energy systems. Under Public Law 119-21, the credit is not allowed for expenditures made after December 31, 2025.
programs.who_its_for: Homeowners reviewing pre-2026 residential clean-energy tax records.
programs.what_its_worth: Expired for post-2025 expenditures; consult a tax professional for prior-year claims.
programs.how_to_start: Use IRS guidance for prior-year questions; do not assume this credit applies to new 2026 work.
programs.source_note: IRS FAQ states section 25D is not allowed for expenditures made after December 31, 2025.

programs.name: Neighborhood Homes Investment Act
programs.plain_summary: The Neighborhood Homes Investment Act would create a federal tax credit for certain neighborhood home development or rehab if Congress enacts it. It is not available to buyers today.
programs.who_its_for: Policy watchers and buyers tracking possible future subsidy.
programs.what_its_worth: Proposed only; no consumer benefit is available unless Congress enacts the bill.
programs.how_to_start: Track H.R. 2854 and S. 1686 on Congress.gov; do not count it in a homebuying budget.
programs.source_note: 119th Congress Neighborhood Homes Investment Act bill pages; proposed, not enacted.
